mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% over 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 100 for 2 consecutive months signals a weakening demand environment that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1.3 million units over 3 months signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 2% and a rise in the CPI above 2.5% over 2 consecutive months signals a monetary policy regime shift that reduces inflation expectations and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 2% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5% over 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining federal funds and rising CPI are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% and a decline in initial claims below 200,000 over 2 consecutive months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 4.5% and PAYEMS < 200,000 over 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment and declining initial claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP below 170 billion over 2 consecutive quarters signals a weakening demand environment that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining real GDP are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1.3 million units and a decline in industrial production below 100 over 2 consecutive months signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.3 million units and INDPRO < 100 over 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts and declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
